review_summary: The transcript is sufficient for diagnostic questions on optical flow and why the temporal stream in two-stream video models can remain surprisingly strong.

question_bank.question: What does optical flow represent in video understanding?
choices: An estimate of how each pixel moves from one frame to the next
The probability that a frame belongs to a class
A set of object bounding boxes over time
A compressed audio representation for the video
question_bank.explanation: Optical flow is an explicit motion representation. For each pixel or location, it estimates a displacement vector showing where that content moves in the next frame.

question_bank.question: Why can the temporal stream in a two-stream network perform surprisingly well?
choices: Motion cues highlight the action itself while removing some distracting appearance and background information
It directly uses the ground-truth labels of adjacent frames
It has access to future test videos during training
It always contains more channels than the RGB stream
question_bank.explanation: Motion representations emphasize how things move rather than what the background looks like. That can make the action signal easier to learn and less prone to overfitting to appearance.
